Horrible Bosses 2 (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $54,445,357Details
International Box Office $51,500,000
Worldwide Box Office $105,945,357
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,282,967
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,826,763
Total $8,109,730
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Fed up by answering to higher-ups, Nick, Dale and Kurt decide to become their own bosses by launching their own business. But a slick investor soon pulls the rug out from under them. Outplayed and desperate, and with no legal resource, the three would-be entrepreneurs hatch a misguided plan to kidnap the investor's adult son and ransom him to regain control of their company.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$15,457,418 (28.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.52 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:51.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,375 opening theaters/3,400 max. theaters, 4.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $79,580,237

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 26th, 2014 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: November 27th, 2014 (Wide) (Germany)
November 28th, 2014 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
November 28th, 2014 (Wide) (Netherlands)
December 12th, 2014 (Wide) (Australia)
December 24th, 2014 (Wide) (France)
Video Release: February 10th, 2015 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for strong crude sexual content and language throughout.
(Rating bulletin 2328, 6/25/2014)
Running Time: 108 minutes
Franchise: Horrible Bosses
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble
Plot point: Betrayal, Boss From Hell, Dysfunctional Family, Kidnap, No Honor Among Thieves, Revenge, Staged Kidnapping, White Collar Crime
Subgenre: Black Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Black Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: RatPac Entertainment, Benderspink Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Fed up by answering to higher-ups, Nick, Dale and Kurt decide to become their own bosses by launching their own business. But a slick investor soon pulls the rug out from under them. Outplayed and desperate, and with no legal resource, the three would-be entrepreneurs hatch a misguided plan to kidnap the investor's adult son and ransom him to regain control of their company.
